TCP/IP.framing: SNIBE simplified-envelope (LIS-174 default): ENQ/STX/payload/ETX/EOT, ACK per control token, no NAK/checksum/frame-numbers/LF. Full E1381-95 framing is used instead only if the analyzer's Online-screen 'Enable Checksum' toggle is on — not yet bench-confirmed which mode a real X3 defaults to (LIS-75/LIS-266).
TCP/IP.notes: Bridge listener bean org.itech.ahb.listen-astm-server.snibe is opt-in (created only when its `port` key is configured) — see edge/drivers configuration.yml. Upload-only: the send/query-response half (LIS-177) is not implemented.

communication.notes: X3 is the TCP client; the bridge is the host (Host ID = 'Lis') per the native Online-interface topology (no SnibeLis middleware on the wire, per the 2026-07-06 owner directive).

default_test_mappings.notes: LIS-299: 14928-6 is the [Moles/volume] (SCnc) code, matching the pmol/L the X3 reports. Do NOT use 3051-0 — that is [Mass/volume] (MCnc), the opposite axis.

LIS-299/LIS-307: the wire code carries a literal ' II' suffix, and the unit is ng/dL — this profile previously declared pmol/L, contradicting the bench capture. 3024-7 is the [Mass/volume] (MCnc) code matching ng/dL. Do NOT use 14920-3 — that is [Moles/volume] (SCnc), the opposite axis.
LIS-299: the axis was already correct ([Units/volume] against uIU/mL); only the wire code gains its literal ' II' suffix.

qcRules.description: WIRE-VERIFIED (LIS-269, 2026-07-24): X3 wraps QC lot+level in '#' in O-3, e.g. '#24725021Q1#'; seen on 5 lots/5 assays, no patient id contains '#'. Replaces inert FIELD_EQUALS O.12=Q: the X3 O-record has only 5 fields, so O.12 never existed.

PROVISIONAL PLACEHOLDER: 'CAL-' is invented, never observed from a real X3, and the 2026-07-24 bench captured no calibration upload. Shipped INACTIVE so it cannot reclassify a patient specimen starting with 'CAL-'; confirm the real convention first.

notes: QC discriminator is BENCH-VERIFIED as of LIS-269 (2026-07-24 client-lab capture, archived under evidence/bench/maglumi-x3/20260724-01010110034012301378/): the SPECIMEN_ID_PATTERN rule is derived from real wire captures across 5 QC lots and 5 assays. It replaces FIELD_EQUALS O.12=Q, which could never fire — the X3 O-record carries only five fields on both QC and patient traffic. The CALIBRATION_* rule remains PROVISIONAL and INACTIVE: no calibration upload has ever been observed. default_test_mappings, by contrast, ARE bench-verified as of v0.2.0 (LIS-272): the three codes and units come from LIS-75 capture session 20260717-0101010034012301113 (raw capture SHA-256 2c50acec48e69f8e24f1abadde11820e284c656a856e74e9a4fca01679900076), and their LOINCs are the axis-corrected set from LIS-299. They replace the earlier synthetic TSH/FT4 seeds, which named the wrong wire codes (no ' II' suffix, and the bridge lookup is exact-match) and put FT4 on the wrong property axis at the wrong unit (LIS-307). The same dictionary is seeded durably in core by liquibase 063 and in the bridge by deploy/kit/compose/bridge-site.yml; all three must stay in step. AFP and CEA are known X3 analytes deliberately left unmapped here (as in the bridge config) pending LIS-38 — do not guess LOINC codes for them. Authoring this profile makes the analyzer_qc_rule / QcRuleDto path provisioned end-to-end (LIS-173) instead of empty, which is what OE's registry full-replace sync (attachQcRules) needs to stop wiping the bridge's QC rules on every sync; it does NOT prove the discriminator is correct on real X3 wire traffic. Until LIS-266 lands, sites running this profile MUST follow the operator QC-review SOP (docs/runbooks/x3-qc-guarded-go-live.md) as the compensating control for QC/calibration misclassification risk. NOTE (LIS-269 bench): a trailing ' II' denotes the SECOND-GENERATION assay and the unsuffixed name the first generation; they are different assays and must never be merged into one mapping. With the QC discriminator now wire-proven, the operator QC-review SOP in docs/runbooks/x3-qc-guarded-go-live.md is no longer the sole control against QC/calibration misclassification, but remains required until the replay proof and validation-owner sign-off land.
